texto: One can be enlightened about proofs as well as theorems. Without enlightenment, one is merely reduced to memorizing proofs. With enlightenment about a proof, its flow becomes clear and it can become an item of astonishing beauty. In addition, the need to memorize disappears because the proof has become part of your soul.
explicacao: Citação de Herbert S. Gaskill sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “One can be enlightened about proofs as well as theorems. Without enlightenment, one is merely red…” articula reflexao, mathematics, about em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
